title: Lesson 7
paragraph: The outdoor course will enable students to examine plants and express their ideas in a notebook. The guide says the walk should last two hours, so students must wear shoes that fit well. They will reduce trash, refuse to touch wild animals, remove litter from the path, settle into groups quickly, and learn how small plants survive in dry weather.

words.meaning: to make it possible for someone to do something
words.example: The new ramp will enable wheelchair users to enter the hall.
The doctor will examine my knee this afternoon.

words.meaning: to show or say what you think or feel
words.example: Students can express their opinions in the survey.

words.meaning: to be the right size or shape
words.example: These shoes fit me better than the old pair.

words.meaning: to continue for a period of time
words.example: The meeting will last about one hour.

words.meaning: to make something smaller or less
words.example: We should reduce food waste at lunch.
You can refuse a plastic bag at the store.

words.meaning: to take something away from a place
words.example: Please remove your shoes before entering the room.

words.meaning: to become calm or make a decision after discussion
words.example: The class will settle into groups before the game starts.

words.meaning: to continue living after danger or difficulty
words.example: Some plants survive with very little water.
